title: rhodanthe collina
intro: Rhodanthe collina, also known as the Hill Billy or Hill Billy daisy, is a perennial plant native to Australia and parts of New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues and skin conditions. Rhodanthe collina can be used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for medicinal use.
